data.narrative:
Skild AI, founded by Deepak Pathak and Abhinav Gupta, is a Series C-stage AI & Software company currently valued at $14.0B+ post-money following $1.4B+ in total capital raised. The peer set comprises four same-sector, same-stage comparables—Figure AI ($39.0B), Luma AI ($4.0B), Runway ($1.5B), and Magic AI ($1.5B)—alongside one public comparable (Palantir) and additional private references including Runway and Figure AI.

Two valuation methods were triangulated. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical Series C-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.7x to the $14.0B last post-money valuation, yielding $23.8B. Peer-median analysis across the four same-stage, same-sector comparables produces a median valuation of $2.8B, with a range spanning $1.5B to $39.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneous risk profiles and revenue trajectories within frontier AI.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $2.3B (low) to $27.4B (high), with a median point estimate of $13.3B. The current $14.0B valuation aligns closely with the median implied value and sits within the upper-middle portion of the range. Key uncertainties include the high variance in peer valuations—a 26x spread between floor and ceiling—and reliance on stage-progression assumptions. The peer-median method's lower implied value ($2.8B) suggests meaningful execution risk relative to investor expectations baked into the $14.0B mark.
